checks.name: format_code_interpretation_1
checks.message: FORMAT-CODE-INTERPRETATION-1: format-code interpretation returns nil for allocated formats (0x00, 0x01) and ErrUnsupportedContentHashFormat for unallocated bytes (§2.3, renamed from PREFIX-DISPATCH-1)
checks.spec_ref: §2.3 normative (FORMAT-CODE-INTERPRETATION-1, renamed from PREFIX-DISPATCH-1: an impl receiving a content_hash whose format-code it does not support returns unsupported_content_hash_format; the format-code is intrinsic to the hash — no dispatch step is distinct from interpreting the leading bytes)

checks.name: authz_delegate_grant_1
checks.message: §9.0 carve-out: targets system/role (extension); core peer 404s before PR-8.2 fires per §6.5 resolution-first. See §A4-AUTHZ scope-exceeds + deny-default for the core surface.
checks.spec_ref: §A4-AUTHZ (AUTHZ-DELEGATE-GRANT-1): delegate without system/role:delegate grant (PR-8.2) MUST surface 403 capability_denied (not 501, not scope_exceeds_authority, not verification_failed)

checks.name: authz_deny_default_1
checks.message: out-of-scope tree:get refused with 403 capability_denied (§A4-AUTHZ default code)
checks.spec_ref: §A4-AUTHZ (AUTHZ-DENY-DEFAULT-1): generic verify_request DENY (cap does not cover op) MUST surface 403 capability_denied as the default authorization code

checks.name: authz_scope_exceeds_1
checks.message: widening cap-request under a narrow presented cap refused with 403 scope_exceeds_authority (§A4-AUTHZ §6.2 specialization)
checks.spec_ref: §A4-AUTHZ (AUTHZ-SCOPE-EXCEEDS-1): §6.2-dispatch-level request exceeds policy/caller authority MUST surface 403 scope_exceeds_authority (not capability_denied default)

checks.name: authz_grantee_1
checks.message: cap with unresolvable grantee refused with 401 unresolvable_grantee (§A4-AUTHZ / PR-3 single 401 carve-out)
checks.spec_ref: §A4-AUTHZ (AUTHZ-GRANTEE-1): cap whose grantee does not resolve to a system/peer entity MUST surface 401 unresolvable_grantee (§5.2 / PR-3 single 401 carve-out — NOT 403 authentication_failed)

checks.name: authz_revoked_1
checks.message: §9.0 carve-out: expects ROLE §5.5 401 capability_revoked (extension vocabulary). Core peer's revocation denial is 403 capability_denied (§5.2 step-4 default); see authz_revoked_core_1.
checks.spec_ref: §A4-AUTHZ (AUTHZ-REVOKED-1) + RULING-CLASS-C (2026-06-11): revoked cap presented on use MUST surface one of (403, capability_revoked) | (403, capability_denied) | (401, capability_revoked); first is RECOMMENDED when verifier has is_revoked semantic, third is the ROLE §5.5 T_old/T_new in-flight cascade carve-out

checks.name: authz_revoked_core_1
checks.message: revoked cap on use refused with 403 capability_denied (§5.2 step-4 default; legitimate core fallback per RULING-CLASS-C)
checks.spec_ref: §9.0 + RULING-CLASS-C (2026-06-11): revoked cap on use MUST surface one of (403, capability_revoked), (403, capability_denied), or (401, capability_revoked); first is RECOMMENDED when verifier has the is_revoked semantic, third is the ROLE §5.5 cascade carve-out

checks.name: authz_no_catchall_1
checks.message: granter unresolvable refused with 403 capability_denied (§A4-AUTHZ AUTHZ-NO-CATCHALL-1 regression pin)
checks.spec_ref: §A4-AUTHZ (AUTHZ-NO-CATCHALL-1): granter identity unresolvable during chain verify MUST surface 403 capability_denied (§5.5 `granter is null → DENY`) — explicit regression pin against the verification_failed catch-all

checks.name: authz_expired_1
checks.message: expired cap on use refused with 403 capability_denied (§A4-AUTHZ — expiry uses the default code, NOT a separate capability_expired string)
checks.spec_ref: §A4-AUTHZ (AUTHZ-EXPIRED-1): cap used after expires_at MUST surface 403 capability_denied as the default code (§5.6 / §5.2 validity — NOT a separate capability_expired string)
